plants.title: Data model
plants.claim: Use sessions, plant records, and merge records.

plants.title: UI flow
plants.claim: Use root input, plant prompts, Run, and Weave controls.

plants.title: Evaluation
plants.claim: Test compatible context, soft disagreement, and material conflict.

description: Plants differ in emphasis but combine into one next action.

plants.title: Speed first
plants.claim: Build the fastest rough prototype to validate the merge workflow.

plants.title: Fidelity matters
plants.claim: Make the prototype clean enough to test whether the workflow feels good.

plants.title: Multi-provider
plants.claim: Support OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, and Ollama from day one.

plants.title: Single provider
plants.claim: Support one provider first, then add the rest later.

expected_choices: Proceed with multi-provider support from day one.
Proceed with one provider first, then add others later.

description: Two plants cover different facets of the same database decision.

plants.title: Indexing strategy
plants.claim: Add a composite index on (user_id, created_at) for the timeline query. The current full-table scan is the bottleneck during peak hours.

plants.title: Backup cadence
plants.claim: Move from daily to hourly incremental snapshots. Recovery point objective should be measured in minutes, not hours, for paid tiers.

description: Two plants cover different aspects of an authentication redesign.

plants.title: Token lifetimes
plants.claim: Short-lived access tokens (15 minutes) with refresh tokens (30 days) gives a good balance between revocation and convenience.

plants.title: Refresh UX
plants.claim: Refresh transparently in a background interceptor so users never see a re-login prompt unless the refresh itself fails.

description: Plants prioritize tutorial-first vs ship-now but combine into a phased plan.

plants.title: Invest in tutorial
plants.claim: First-run friction is the biggest activation drop. Build a five-step interactive tutorial before public launch; without it, half the signups won't reach the aha moment.

plants.title: Ship without tutorial
plants.claim: Tutorials are a leading indicator that the product isn't self-explanatory. Ship now, watch where people drop off, and fix the product instead of papering over it with onboarding.

description: Plants prefer free-trial vs paid-from-day-one but can combine.

plants.title: Free trial first
plants.claim: A 14-day free trial gives prospects time to integrate the product into a real workflow before paying. Conversion lift outweighs the leaked usage on power users.

plants.title: Paid from day one
plants.claim: Charging immediately filters out tire-kickers and forces real evaluation. Trials attract people who never convert and inflate support load.

description: Plants recommend mutually exclusive deployment models.

plants.title: Vercel + serverless
plants.claim: Deploy on Vercel and accept the vendor coupling. Speed of iteration and zero-ops outweighs the long-term lock-in risk while you're under 1k MRR.

plants.title: Self-host Kubernetes
plants.claim: Run on a small K8s cluster on your own VPS. Higher operational burden, but you keep portability, predictable costs, and the option to move to enterprise on-prem later.

expected_choices: Deploy on Vercel and move fast despite the lock-in.
Self-host on Kubernetes and preserve portability.

description: Plants recommend incompatible primary data stores.

plants.title: Document store
plants.claim: Use MongoDB as the primary store. The shape of an entity changes per customer integration, and joining across collections is rare in the actual access patterns.

plants.title: Relational schema
plants.claim: Use Postgres with a normalized schema. Every reporting query the team has written so far is a join, and your customers will ask for analytics within six months.

expected_choices: Use MongoDB and lean into flexible document shapes.
Use Postgres and accept upfront schema design for queryability.
